analysis.executive_summary: February 2026 showed a modest net operating result of $44,000, but tuition collection fell below target at 93.00% (vs. 94.00% threshold). Payroll expenses exceeded budget by 4.0% despite being 39% of revenue. The primary risk is delayed tuition collections impacting cash flow, with no overdue invoices reported but transaction evidence unavailable.
key_findings: Tuition collection rate at 93.00% missed the 94.00% target by 1.00 percentage point
Payroll expenses exceeded budget by $30,677.22 (4.0% variance) despite being 39% of revenue
All departments showed revenue variance (-3%) and expense variance (+2.06%)
No overdue invoices reported despite collection rate shortfall
root_causes: Unverified transaction data preventing root cause analysis of collection shortfall
Payroll budgeting may not align with actual headcount or compensation changes
Departmental expense overruns (2.06% average) may indicate operational inefficiencies

recommendations.action: Investigate transaction processing system for missing payment records
recommendations.rationale: Collection rate shortfall requires understanding why $1,369,431 was paid against $1,472,507 due
recommendations.supporting_evidence: Evidence package STEP-001 failed to retrieve processed transactions
recommendations.expected_impact: Identify if collections are delayed or system errors are causing shortfall

recommendations.action: Review payroll budget vs. actual headcount and compensation changes
recommendations.rationale: Payroll variance of 4.0% requires understanding if budgeting assumptions were accurate
recommendations.expected_impact: Adjust payroll budgeting methodology to prevent future overruns

recommendations.action: Analyze departmental expense overruns for operational inefficiencies
recommendations.rationale: All departments showed consistent expense overruns (2.06% average)
recommendations.expected_impact: Identify cost-saving opportunities without impacting revenue

strategic_priorities: Fix tuition collection process to meet 94% target
Align payroll budgeting with actual headcount and compensation
missing_information: Transaction data showing payment processing status
Headcount and compensation changes affecting payroll budget
analysis.reasoning_summary: Collection rate shortfall is the primary risk (93% vs 94% target), but evidence for root cause is missing (STEP-001 failed). Payroll variance is significant but explainable by budgeting methodology. Departmental expense overruns are consistent but not critical. Missing transaction data prevents full analysis of collection issue.
